title: Design System: Care Bridge Home Wellness App

greeting.purpose: The personal 'Good morning, Anita' home header. Largest, warmest text in the app; appears once per screen.

heading-l.purpose: Top-bar page titles (Medications, Trackers, Daily Check-in).

stat.purpose: The one-focal-number pattern: wellness score, heart rate, adherence %, tracker latest values.

body.purpose: Standard copy, form inputs, list titles.

overline.purpose: Uppercase section headers and medication time-of-day band labels.

shadows.name: xs
shadows.purpose: Default resting state for secondary cards.

shadows.name: md
shadows.purpose: Primary/hero card lift — exactly one per screen.

shadows.name: lg
shadows.purpose: Floating elements: FAB, toasts, bottom sheets.

shadows.name: sos-glow
shadows.purpose: Emergency SOS button glow — the one element allowed to visually shout.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions and view changes.

motion.name: reveal-rise
motion.purpose: Staggered card entrance on view activation; disabled entirely under prefers-reduced-motion.

breakpoints.name: device
breakpoints.purpose: iPhone-frame width this app is designed at; not a responsive breakpoint system — single fixed mobile viewport.

components.name: Primary CTA Button
components.description: The single primary action per screen, e.g. 'Log today's check-in'.
components.html: <button class="ds-mbtn-fill"><svg class="ds-icon" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="12" y1="5" x2="12" y2="19"/><line x1="5" y1="12" x2="19" y2="12"/></svg>Log today's check-in</button>
components.css: .ds-mbtn-fill{ display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:8px; width:100%; padding:14px 18px; border-radius:14px; font-family:'Urbanist',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:15px; background:#3E6FB8; color:#fff; transition:filter .15s, transform .12s; } .ds-mbtn-fill .ds-icon{ width:19px; height:19px; } .ds-mbtn-fill:hover{ filter:brightness(.96); } .ds-mbtn-fill:active{ transform:scale(.98); } .ds-mbtn-fill:focus-visible{ outline:3px solid #CFE0F4; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Emergency SOS Button
components.description: The one button allowed to visually shout — danger red with a glow shadow.
components.css: .ds-mbtn-sos{ display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; width:100%; padding:17px; border-radius:14px; background:#E5484D; color:#fff; box-shadow:0 10px 26px -6px rgba(229,72,77,.6); transition:background .15s, transform .12s; } .ds-mbtn-sos .ds-icon{ width:21px; height:21px; flex-shrink:0; } .ds-sos-text{ display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:flex-start; font-family:'Urbanist',sans-serif; } .ds-sos-title{ font-size:16px; font-weight:800; letter-spacing:.3px; } .ds-sos-sub{ font-size:11.5px; font-weight:500; opacity:.9; } .ds-mbtn-sos:hover{ background:#B4282D; } .ds-mbtn-sos:active{ transform:scale(.98); }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Pairs a tinted background with matching text — never color alone.
components.html: Taken
components.css: .ds-chip{ display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:5px; padding:4px 10px; border-radius:999px; font-family:'Urbanist',sans-serif; font-size:12px; font-weight:600; line-height:1; } .ds-chip .ds-icon{ width:13px; height:13px; } .ds-chip-ok{ background:#E6FBF3; color:#0B7357; }

components.name: Wellness Score Ring
components.description: Signature single-metric ring, reused for wellness score, adherence %, heart rate.
components.css: .ds-ring{ width:68px; height:68px; border-radius:50%; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; position:relative; background:conic-gradient(#0E9670 0% 85%, #E9E8E9 85% 100%); } .ds-ring::after{ content:''; position:absolute; inset:7px; border-radius:50%; background:linear-gradient(145deg,#f5f0fa,#fff); } .ds-ring-num{ position:relative; z-index:1; font-family:'Urbanist',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:22px; color:#0E9670; }

components.name: Time-of-Day Medication Band
components.description: Signature Medications-screen component — warm full-bleed tint per time-of-day group.
components.html:
Morning
Levothyroxine 50mcg
Empty stomach · Taken 7:30 AM
components.css: .ds-tod-band{ border-radius:16px; padding:12px 14px; background:#FCF3E2; } .ds-tod-label{ font-family:'Urbanist',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:11.5px; letter-spacing:.4px; text-transform:uppercase; color:#92580A; margin-bottom:8px; } .ds-tod-row{ display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px; padding:7px 0; } .ds-tod-check{ width:19px; height:19px; accent-color:#3E6FB8; } .ds-tod-name{ font-weight:600; font-size:13.5px; color:#151632; text-decoration:line-through; opacity:.6; } .ds-tod-note{ font-size:11.5px; color:#6A6B80; margin-top:1px; }

narrative.overview: This is the Self Care face of Care Bridge Home — the one member-facing surface in the whole platform, and the only one built for someone who isn't a trained operator. Where the Admin Portal earns trust through operational gravity and the Field app through speed, the Wellness App earns trust through steadiness: it shows up the same way every day, never demands urgency it hasn't earned, and never makes a 68-year-old member feel like they're operating machinery. It explicitly rejects the two easy failure modes for a healthcare app: the cold clinical patient portal and the gamified consumer wellness app. The palette shares its bones with the Admin Portal but swaps the operating accent from admin purple to Reassurance Blue — calmer, softer, less 'control room,' more 'someone is looking out for you.' Icons run bold (2.2px stroke) because this audience needs glanceable clarity more than any other face of the product.
keyCharacteristics: Reassurance Blue as the single operating accent; Self Care lavender appears only as the hero card's model-identity banner
Soft-lifted primary cards vs. flat-bordered secondary cards — a deliberate two-tier elevation hierarchy
Time-of-day medication bands in warm, distinguishable tints
Status always paired with a label or icon, never color alone

rules.name: The One-Purpose-Accent Rule
rules.body: Reassurance Blue is the only color that means 'act here' or 'this is active.' Self Care Lavender identifies a care tier, full stop.

rules.name: The Never-Color-Alone Rule
rules.body: Every status pairs color with a label, icon, or strikethrough — never color alone.

rules.name: The One Number Per Glance Rule
rules.body: Every card has at most one Stat-weight (22px) number as its focal point.

rules.name: The Two-Tier Lift Rule
rules.body: Exactly one card per screen gets shadow-md; everything else rests at shadow-xs.

dos: Do use Reassurance Blue for the single primary action per screen, active nav state, and focus rings — nothing else.
Do give exactly one card per screen shadow-md; everything else rests at shadow-xs.
Do pair every status color with a label, icon, or strikethrough. Never color alone.
Do keep touch targets ≥44×44px and icon stroke-width ≥2.2px.
Do use Self Care Lavender only for Care Model identity, never for buttons or active states.
donts: Don't treat this like the Admin Portal — no dense multi-column layouts, no data tables, no urgency-first ordering.
Don't build a generic consumer fitness-app dashboard. Every metric should trace back to a watching care team.
Don't make this feel like a clinical patient portal — no dense forms, no bureaucratic tone.
Don't let two competing Stat-weight numbers appear on the same card.
Don't use border-left as a colored accent stripe.
Don't reintroduce the neon #00FFB0/#00DBFF field/family accent pair — removed from css/mobile.css; canonical accents live in css/tokens.css only.
